title: brassica oleracea var. ligustica
intro: Brassica oleracea var. ligustica, also known as Italian or wild broccoli, is a variety of the Brassica oleracea plant that belongs to the mustard family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. The health benefits of Italian broccoli include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and support immune function. Italian broccoli can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, capsules, and tinctures, although it is often used fresh in salads or sautéed as a side dish.
